overview.description: Medical AI startup building algorithms and digital tools for Czech healthcare
overview.description_long: Mild Blue builds AI-powered software for hospitals and clinical laboratories. Their flagship product slp.blue® modernises lab documentation and quality management for 45+ hospitals; other tools include HealthWarden (remote patient monitoring), TX Matching (kidney transplant optimisation), Breviary (drug dosage calculator), and Medical OCR. The team is highly technical — all four co-founders have mathematics backgrounds from Czech Technical University (ČVUT).
overview.tagline: We create algorithms that save lives

history.origin_story: Founded in March 2015 by four mathematicians from ČVUT (Czech Technical University). Focused exclusively on healthcare AI — building tools that help doctors and lab staff work smarter rather than optimising ads or insurance.

key_milestones.event: GitHub organisation created; team begins scaling open-source medical tooling
TX Matching tool deployed at IKEM (leading Czech transplant hospital) and expanded to Israel and Austria
slp.blue® trusted by ~100 laboratories in CZ and abroad; exhibiting at major clinical biochemistry conferences

offices.notes: Operational HQ near Anděl metro (TJ Sokol building, Smíchov)

risk_assessment.agi_displacement_rationale: Deep domain specialisation in Czech/Slovak clinical laboratory workflows with ISO-compliant documentation, EHR integrations, and multi-country regulatory compliance. Foundation models cannot easily replicate these domain-specific integrations and the trust built with 45+ hospital partners over a decade.
risk_assessment.market_risk_rationale: Real, paying customers in a regulated market with strong structural demand (Czech eHealth mandates, EU healthcare digitisation). 45 hospitals and ~100 labs represent proven PMF and recurring subscription revenue.
risk_assessment.funding_risk_rationale: No known external funding — appears bootstrapped. With 21 staff and revenue from 45+ hospitals, the company likely covers costs, but limited war chest constrains international expansion speed. Jobs page indicates planned westward European expansion.
risk_assessment.overall_risk_rationale: Strong domain moat (regulatory, integration, trust with Czech healthcare), proven paying customer base, and a decade of institutional knowledge. Bootstrapped nature is a mild constraint but the niche is defensible. Low AGI displacement risk due to regulatory and integration complexity.

metadata.notes: Company legally registered in Plzeň but operationally based in Prague (Smíchov/Anděl). 21 people on team page; jobs page states ~12 full-time core staff. GitHub org created 2020; Czech ARES confirms founding date 2015-03-09.
